name: damp-floor-level-stain
description: There is a dark stain at the base of my interior wall. It's been there for months and seems to be growing.

hypotheses.label: Rising damp (no DPC)
evidence_for: Stain at floor level consistent with rising water
Months of growth matches slow wicking

hypotheses.label: Leaking pipe behind wall
evidence_for: Interior wall location could conceal a pipe
evidence_against: Pipe leak usually has more defined wet patch not diffuse stain

hypotheses.label: Lateral seepage from outside
evidence_against: Would typically worsen after rain, not be constant

mockAgentOutput.what_we_dont_know: We cannot tell whether the stain gets worse after rain or stays constant regardless of weather.
mockAgentOutput.why_it_matters: If it worsens after rain, the source is external penetration or seepage. If constant, the source is internal — either a pipe or rising ground moisture.

chips.text: It gets worse after heavy rain.
It stays the same rain or shine.

expected.must_ask_about: whether stain worsens after rain or stays constant
